diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: An electrophysiologic study (EPS) revealed an intra-nodal dual pathway causing AVNRT. The complication of a tiny atrial septal defect (ASD) was initially suspected as the cause, but it was ruled out due to a very faint shunt flow and no induction of hypoxemia during a round-off back somersault. The major triggering mechanisms were speculated to be related to transient atrial overload and autonomic imbalance induced by a swift postural-axial change together with an intense Valsalva maneuver with the maximal level of breath holding.
